questions.question: Which of the following commands can be used to link to a page directly within a paragraph?

options.text: /link
options.explanation: The /link command creates a 'Link to page' block, not an inline link.
Typing @ allows you to search for and link to a page inline.

options.text: Ctrl+K
options.explanation: Ctrl+K is a browser shortcut for opening links, not a Notion command for linking pages.
The # symbol is used for creating new headings, not linking to pages.

questions.question: How do you create a link to a specific block within a page?

options.text: Copy the text of the block and paste it elsewhere.
options.explanation: Copying text loses the block's specific anchor ID needed for linking.

options.text: Hover over the block and click the ⋮⋮ menu, then select 'Copy link to block'.
options.explanation: This action copies the specific URL anchor for that block, which can be pasted anywhere.

options.text: Use the /link command and type the block's name.
options.explanation: The /link command links to pages, not individual blocks.

options.text: Drag the block to a new location to generate a link.
options.explanation: Dragging moves the block; it does not generate a link to it.

questions.question: What happens when you paste a web page's URL and select 'Paste as mention'?

options.text: It opens the page in a new browser tab.
options.explanation: A mention creates a compact, clickable link in the page itself, not a new tab.

options.text: It creates a link with the icon, source, and title of the page.
options.explanation: Link mentions render a preview card containing the source icon, title, and source.

options.text: It embeds the full web page content into Notion.
options.explanation: Embeds are different from link mentions; mentions only show a preview card.

options.text: It converts the URL into a text link without any formatting.
options.explanation: This would defeat the purpose of a link mention, which is meant to look nice and clean.

questions.question: What distinguishes a 'Link to page' block from an @-mention?

options.text: A Link to page block creates a sub-page in the sidebar.
options.explanation: Unlike @-mentions, Link to page blocks appear in the sidebar as sub-pages of the current page.

options.text: @-mentions create a sub-page in the sidebar.
options.explanation: @-mentions act like standard hyperlinks and do not create sidebar sub-pages.

options.text: Link to page blocks cannot be moved once created.
options.explanation: Link to page blocks can be moved using the ⋮⋮ menu and drag functionality.

options.text: @-mentions allow you to see the properties of the linked page.
options.explanation: While @-mentions link to the page, they do not automatically display its properties like a sub-page.

questions.question: How do you customize backlinks for an entire database page?

options.text: Click the ••• menu, select 'Customize page', and adjust the Backlinks dropdown.
options.explanation: This method applies to non-database pages, not database pages.

options.text: Click the ••• menu, select 'Customize layout', open the Heading module, and adjust the dropdown.
options.explanation: In databases, you must go to Customize layout, open the Heading module, and select 'Apply to all pages'.

options.text: Select 'Backlinks' from the Properties tab and toggle the switch.
options.explanation: Backlinks are a display feature in the page body, not a toggleable property in the database properties.

options.text: You cannot customize backlinks for database pages; they are static.
options.explanation: You can fully customize backlinks for database pages to show, hide, or show on hover only.

questions.question: Where can you find the backlinks section for a specific page?

options.text: In the page's sidebar properties.
options.explanation: While database pages show backlinks in properties, standard pages show them under the title.

options.text: Under the page title, where you can select '{#} backlinks'.
options.explanation: You can select '{#} backlinks' directly under the page title to view all incoming links.

options.text: At the very bottom of the page content area.
options.explanation: Backlinks are not located at the bottom of the page content.

options.text: In the desktop app's 'Open backlinks' settings.
options.explanation: Backlinks are a feature of the page view, not a setting in the desktop app.

questions.question: Which of the following is a requirement for a web page link to render rich information?

options.text: The link must be longer than 50 characters.
options.explanation: Link mention previews work regardless of the URL length.

options.text: The web page must be a public page accessible without authentication.
options.explanation: Link mentions work best with public pages; pages requiring login/auth often fail to render rich info.

options.text: The Notion user must have a paid subscription.
options.explanation: Link mentions are available to all Notion users.

options.text: The link must be pasted as a block, not text.
options.explanation: Link mentions work best when pasted as text/mention rather than a block.

questions.question: What happens if you attempt to view backlinks for a private page that you do not own?

options.text: You will see all the backlinks, but they will be labeled 'Private'.
options.explanation: You generally cannot see backlinks to pages you do not have access to.

options.text: You will see a generic 'Backlinks' section with no data.
options.explanation: Access restrictions prevent viewing the data entirely.

options.text: You will not be able to see the backlinks section at all.
options.explanation: You will only see backlinks for pages you have access to. Backlinks to private pages you don't own are hidden.

options.text: The backlinks will appear, but they will be marked as 'Hidden'.
options.explanation: There is no 'Hidden' label; the data is simply inaccessible.

questions.question: How can you move a 'Link to page' block within a page?

options.text: Click the text of the link and drag it to a new location.
options.explanation: You must use the block menu, not the text, to move the block.

options.text: Hover over the block and drag the text.
options.explanation: Dragging the text does not move the block itself.

options.text: Click the ⋮⋮ to the left of the block and drag it to your desired location.
options.explanation: The block menu allows you to drag the block element to reorder it.

options.text: Edit the link URL and press Enter to move it.
options.explanation: Editing the URL changes the destination, not the position of the block.

questions.question: Where is the setting located to configure opening Notion links in the desktop app?

options.text: In the Notion desktop app's 'Preferences' menu.
options.explanation: You must configure this in the web browser settings, not the desktop app settings.

options.text: In the Notion web app's 'Settings' -> 'Preferences'.
options.explanation: Navigate to Notion in your web browser, then Settings -> Preferences to toggle the setting.

options.text: In the Notion Help Center article 'Open links in desktop app'.
options.explanation: The article describes *how* to do it, but the actual setting is in the Preferences menu.

options.text: In the system-level browser settings.
options.explanation: Notion has a specific toggle within its own Preferences menu for this behavior.

questions.question: Which method creates a 'Link to page' block that appears in the sidebar?

options.text: Typing @ and selecting the page in the menu.
options.explanation: @-mentions do not create sidebar sub-pages.

options.text: Typing /link and selecting the page in the menu.
options.explanation: Using the /link command creates a block that acts as a sub-page in the sidebar.

options.text: Copying the page's URL and pasting it as a link.
options.explanation: Pasting a URL creates a link mention, not a sidebar sub-page block.

options.text: Dragging the page icon from the sidebar to the content.
options.explanation: You cannot drag sidebar icons into the content to create link blocks.

options.text: A link to a Notion block within a paragraph.
options.explanation: Link mentions refer to web pages, not Notion blocks.

options.text: A compact link to a web page that displays a preview card.
options.explanation: Link mentions are a specific feature for pasting web URLs to generate clean, previewed links.

options.text: A type of database property used to track incoming links.
options.explanation: Backlinks are the property used to track incoming links, not link mentions.

options.text: A command to automatically hide backlinks.
options.explanation: Link mentions are about displaying web content, not hiding backlinks.
